prose:
**Fukui electrophilicity index (most electrophilic site, atom 3/C) — DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.**
Geometry: fixed input coordinates from the provided file `phenol.xyz`; no additional optimisation performed (single-point evaluations on neutral, N–1 cation, and N+1 anion geometries).  The calculation used Mulliken charges to assemble the condensed Fukui functions; the largest f⁺ value identifies C-3 as the most electrophilic atom.

Live run details: the job was executed with
`cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 phenol.xyz`, streaming PySCF output to a timestamped `.out` file announced at launch and writing results to `phenol_fukui_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json`.

SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity status `ok`, trustworthy = true.  No solvent was specified, so the calculation ran in the gas phase (no implicit solvation).  Density fitting (RI/RI-JK) was **not** employed.

Returned highlights (Mulliken-condensed indices; units dimensionless):
• f⁺ (nucleophilic attack) sum = 1.0000; largest value 2.1028319 on atom 3 (C).
• f⁻ (electrophilic attack) sum = 1.0000; largest value 0.16201477 on atom 1 (O).
• Dual descriptor indicates C-3 is strongly electrophilic, O is the most nucleophilic.

All warnings from chemkit are reproduced verbatim above; none were suppressed. The result is therefore considered computational and, per engine report, trustworthy.
